text.title: Spring Async Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real Spring Boot deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Method annotated @Async appears to run synchronously; thread name matches caller; logs show no executor activity.
antipattern.root-cause: Caller calls `this.asyncMethod()` inside the same @Service class; proxy interception skipped.
antipattern.fix: Extract the async method to a dedicated @Service bean (e.g., AsyncEmailService); inject and call from the original class.

antipattern.symptom: Production thread count grows without bound; eventually JVM/OS thread limit hit; OOM-killer or RejectedExecutionException.
antipattern.root-cause: @Async with no argument falls back to SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor — new OS thread per invocation.
antipattern.fix: Define a named ThreadPoolTaskExecutor bean with bounded pool + queue; reference by name in every @Async.

antipattern.symptom: Background tasks silently fail; no error metric, no log, customer sees missing email.

root-cause.source: Spring Framework reference — async exception handling

antipattern.root-cause: void-returning @Async with no AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler — exception path silenced.
antipattern.fix: Return CompletableFuture<Void> + log .exceptionally OR register AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler that logs + increments Micrometer counter.

antipattern.symptom: Async method reads uncommitted data; intermittent ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException; data inconsistency.
antipattern.root-cause: @Async + @Transactional on the same method — the async thread has no JDBC connection from the caller's tx.
antipattern.fix: Remove @Transactional from the async method; either caller commits before scheduling async, or async opens its own tx.

antipattern.symptom: Deploys drop emails / webhook deliveries; tail of audit log is missing.
antipattern.root-cause: ThreadPoolTaskExecutor lacks setWaitForTasksToCompleteOnShutdown(true) — pool killed immediately on SIGTERM.
antipattern.fix: Set setWaitForTasksToCompleteOnShutdown(true) + setAwaitTerminationSeconds(60); for durability, route to a queue.
